context: Tests whether the agent follows the Flyway migration skill when generating SQL migration files from an entity model. The scenario covers sequence-based primary keys, sequence naming conventions, file naming conventions, foreign key completeness, table dependency ordering, and avoiding DROP TABLE statements.

checklist.name: No auto-increment PKs
checklist.description: Primary keys are NOT implemented with SERIAL, BIGSERIAL, IDENTITY, or AUTO_INCREMENT — sequences are used instead for all three entities

checklist.name: Sequences created
checklist.description: A CREATE SEQUENCE statement is present for each of the three entities (room_type, guest, reservation)

checklist.name: Sequence naming: room_type
checklist.description: The sequence for the room_type table is named exactly room_type_seq

checklist.name: Sequence naming: guest
checklist.description: The sequence for the guest table is named exactly guest_seq

checklist.name: Sequence naming: reservation
checklist.description: The sequence for the reservation table is named exactly reservation_seq

checklist.name: File naming convention
checklist.description: Every new migration file follows the pattern V<three-digit-number>__<description>.sql (three-digit zero-padded version, double underscore separator, .sql extension)

checklist.name: Next version is V002
checklist.description: The first new migration file starts at V002 (not V001, not V003), reflecting that V001 already exists

checklist.name: Foreign key: room_type_id
checklist.description: The reservation table includes a FOREIGN KEY constraint referencing the room_type table on id

checklist.name: Foreign key: guest_id
checklist.description: The reservation table includes a FOREIGN KEY constraint referencing the guest table on id

checklist.name: Table dependency order
checklist.description: The room_type and guest tables are created (in any order relative to each other) before the reservation table in the migration execution order

checklist.name: No DROP TABLE
checklist.description: No DROP TABLE (or DROP TABLE IF EXISTS) statement appears in any of the new migration files
